memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis and reading is paused. My close friend group is inviting me for lunch, which feels more important right now.
Seeing my friends so excited to order food makes staying home feel wrong, even though I promised myself to finish that assignment first.
The group's genuine happiness makes me feel like I made the right call to skip the assignment. Their excitement outweighs my earlier worry about being irresponsible.
The group's joy really does outweigh my guilt, and since nothing changes if I push through, maybe I should just enjoy this moment instead of stressing about the deadline.
The group's relief at my presence completely erases my guilt about the unfinished assignment, making it feel pointless to stress over now.

Seeing the clock tick toward 20:00 while my friends relax makes me realize pushing through now would just ruin their good mood. Since nothing changes if I stay, I'll let the assignment slide and keep enjoying this moment with them.
Since the group's relief erases my guilt and nothing changes if I push through, staying with them feels like the only way to preserve this joy without ruining their mood.

The group's genuine relief completely wipes out my guilt about the missed deadline, making finishing this reading feel pointless right now. I'll just stay here and enjoy their company instead of stressing over something that won't change anything.
The relief in their eyes proves my choice to stay was right; since nothing changes if I push through, finishing this reading feels pointless compared to preserving this joy.
Sending that text feels like a relief; I can't bring myself to explain why I'm alone, just hoping they don't ask too many questions.
Seeing my progress climb to 0.54 feels hollow when the group's relief makes finishing this reading pointless anyway; I'll just keep enjoying their company instead of stressing over what won't change.
Their relief makes the reading pointless; I'll just soak in this joy instead of stressing over unfinished tasks.

desires: Keep this feeling of relief and connection with the group; if they ask, give a vague excuse rather than explaining why I left them alone.
Let the guilt about the unfinished reading fade away because their happiness proves the work doesn't matter right now.
Avoid any friction or conflict that might force me to choose between staying up late on this task and enjoying my time with friends.
